source: Schubert, T., Friedmann, F., & Regenbrecht, H. (2001). The experience of presence: Factor analytic insights. Presence: Teleoperators and Virtual Environments, 10(3), 266-281.
description: The IPQ is a self-report questionnaire measuring the sense of presence in virtual environments. It yields a General Presence score and three subscale scores.

response_scale.note: Some items use different bipolar anchor pairs; see item-level notes.

SP.label: Spatial Presence
SP.description: The sense of being physically located inside the virtual environment.
SP.scoring: Mean of item scores (after reversing any reverse-coded items). Higher = stronger spatial presence.

INV.label: Involvement
INV.description: The degree to which attention is captured and the user is involved with the VE.
INV.scoring: Mean of item scores. Higher = greater involvement.

REAL.label: Experienced Realism
REAL.description: The degree to which the virtual environment is perceived as realistic.
REAL.scoring: Mean of item scores. Higher = greater perceived realism.

general_presence_item.text: In the computer generated world I had a sense of 'being there'.
general_presence_item.note: Single global item used as a standalone general presence indicator or for validity checks.

items.text: I had a sense of 'being there' in the virtual environment.
Somehow I felt that the virtual world surrounded me.
I felt present in the virtual space.
It seemed as if I actually took part in the action of the virtual environment.
I had the impression of moving through the virtual environment.
I noticed the virtual environment through more than one sense.
I had the impression that the virtual world was real.
How aware were you of the real world while navigating in the virtual world?
items.note: Reverse-coded: high real-world awareness implies low presence/involvement.

items.text: How real did the virtual world seem to you?
How much did your experience in the virtual environment seem consistent with your real-world experience?
The virtual environment seemed real to me.
The virtual world seemed more realistic than the real world.

composite_scoring.formula: Each subscale score = mean of its items (reverse-coded items recoded first as: recoded = 8 - raw). Overall IPQ profile is reported as three subscale means plus the single G item.
composite_scoring.reporting: Report SP, INV, REAL means separately; do not collapse to a single composite unless explicitly justified.

psychometric_properties.validity: Convergent validity established against Slater-Usoh-Steed (SUS) scale. Discriminant validity against cybersickness (SSQ).
psychometric_properties.factor_structure: Three-factor CFA; SP = physical-space presence, INV = attentional involvement, REAL = plausibility/realism.
